--- title: Excitation models format: html: toc: true number-sections: true engine: julia execute: freeze: auto cache: true --- ## Available models To define an excitation, two steps must be followed: 1. Initialize an excitation type: ```julia exc_type = MyExcitation(params...) ``` 2. Compute the excitation signal corresponding to the given excitation type over a given duration `t` (See @sec-exc-api for details): ```julia F = excitation(exc_type, t) ``` ### Rectangular signal ::: {.api} **Rectangle** --- ::: {#4 .cell execution_count=1} ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ```{=html} 
Rectangle(F₀, tstart, duration)

Struct to define a rectangular excitation signal

Fields

  • F₀: Amplitude of the force [N]

  • tstart: Starting time of the excitation [s]

  • duration: Duration of the excitation [s]

``` ::: ::: ::: ::: {#6 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.julia .cell-code} # Time parameters Δt = 1e-3 t = 0.:Δt:10. # Excitation parameters F₀ = 1. tstart = 2.5 duration = 5. # Initialize Rectangle type rect = Rectangle(F₀, tstart, duration) # Compute the excitation F = excitation(rect, t) # Display fig = Figure() ax = Axis(fig[1, 1], xlabel = "Time (s)", ylabel = "Excitation signal", title = "Rectangle") lines!(ax, t, F) display(fig); ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](excitation_files/figure-html/cell-4-output-1.svg){} ::: ::: ### Triangle signal ::: {.api} **Triangle** --- ::: {#8 .cell execution_count=1} ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ```{=html} 
Triangle(F₀, tstart, duration)

Struct to define a triangular excitation signal

Fields

  • F₀: Amplitude of the force [N]

  • tstart: Starting time of the excitation [s]

  • duration: Duration of the excitation [s]

``` ::: ::: ::: ::: {#10 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.julia .cell-code} # Time parameters Δt = 1e-3 t = 0.:Δt:10. # Excitation parameters F₀ = 1. tstart = 2.5 duration = 5. # Initialize Triangle type tri = Triangle(F₀, tstart, duration) # Compute the excitation F = excitation(tri, t) # Display fig = Figure() ax = Axis(fig[1, 1], xlabel = "Time (s)", ylabel = "Excitation signal", title = "Triangle") lines!(ax, t, F) display(fig); ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](excitation_files/figure-html/cell-6-output-1.svg){} ::: ::: ### Hammer impact ::: {.api} **Hammer** --- ::: {#12 .cell execution_count=1} ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ```{=html} 
Hammer(F₀, tstart, k, θ)

Struct to define a hammer impact excitation signal

Fields

  • F₀: Amplitude of the force [N]

  • tstart: Starting time of the excitation [s]

  • k: Shape parameter

  • θ: Intensity parameter [s]

``` ::: ::: ::: ::: {#14 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.julia .cell-code} # Time parameters Δt = 1e-5 t = 0.:Δt:0.05 # Excitation parameters F₀ = 1. tstart = 0.01 k = 9.7 θ = 6e-4 # Initialize Hammer type hammer = Hammer(F₀, tstart, k, θ) # Compute the excitation F = excitation(hammer, t) # Display fig = Figure() ax = Axis(fig[1, 1], xlabel = "Time (s)", ylabel = "Excitation signal", title = "Hammer") lines!(ax, t, F) display(fig); ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](excitation_files/figure-html/cell-8-output-1.svg){} ::: ::: ### Smooth rectangular signal ::: {.api} **SmoothRect** --- ::: {#16 .cell execution_count=1} ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ```{=html} 
SmoothRect(F₀, tstart, tr, duration)

Struct to define a smooth rectangular excitation signal

Fields

  • F₀: Amplitude of the force [N]

  • tstart: Starting time of the excitation [s]

  • duration: Duration of the excitation [s]

  • trise: Rise time from 0 to F₀ [s]

``` ::: ::: ::: ::: {#18 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.julia .cell-code} # Time parameters Δt = 1e-3 t = 0.:Δt:10. # Excitation parameters F₀ = 1. tstart = 2.5 duration = 5. trise = 1. # Initialize SmoothRect type srect = SmoothRect(F₀, tstart, duration, trise) # Compute the excitation F = excitation(srect, t) # Display fig = Figure() ax = Axis(fig[1, 1], xlabel = "Time (s)", ylabel = "Excitation signal", title = "SmoothRect") lines!(ax, t, F) display(fig); ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](excitation_files/figure-html/cell-10-output-1.svg){} ::: ::: ### Sine signal ::: {.api} **SineWave** --- ::: {#20 .cell execution_count=1} ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ```{=html} 
SineWave(F₀, tstart, duration, freq; zero_end = true)

Struct to define a sine wave excitation signal

Constructor parameters

  • F₀: Amplitude of the force [N]

  • tstart: Starting time of the excitation [s]

  • duration: Duration of the excitation [s]

  • freq: Frequency of the excitation [Hz]

  • zero_end: Boolean to set the excitation to 0 at the end of the duration (default = true)

Fields

  • F₀: Amplitude of the force [N]

  • tstart: Starting time of the excitation [s]

  • duration: Duration of the excitation [s]

  • ω: Frequency of the excitation [Hz]

  • zero_end: Boolean to set the excitation to 0 at the end of the duration (default = true)

``` ::: ::: ::: ::: {#22 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.julia .cell-code} # Time parameters Δt = 1e-3 t = 0.:Δt:10. # Excitation parameters F₀ = 1. tstart = 2.5 duration = 5. f = 2. # Initialize HaverSine type sine = SineWave(F₀, tstart, duration, f, zero_end = true) # Compute the excitation F = excitation(sine, t) # Display fig = Figure() ax = Axis(fig[1, 1], xlabel = "Time (s)", ylabel = "Excitation signal", title = "SineWave") lines!(ax, t, F) display(fig); ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](excitation_files/figure-html/cell-12-output-1.svg){} ::: ::: ### Half-sine signal ::: {.api} **HalfSine** --- ::: {#24 .cell execution_count=1} ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ```{=html} 
HalfSine(F₀, tstart, duration)

Struct to define a half sine excitation signal

Fields

  • F₀: Amplitude of the force [N]

  • tstart: Starting time of the excitation [s]

  • duration: Duration of the excitation [s]

``` ::: ::: ::: ::: {#26 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.julia .cell-code} # Time parameters Δt = 1e-3 t = 0.:Δt:10. # Excitation parameters F₀ = 1. tstart = 2.5 duration = 5. # Initialize HalfSine type hsine = HalfSine(F₀, tstart, duration) # Compute the excitation F = excitation(hsine, t) # Display fig = Figure() ax = Axis(fig[1, 1], xlabel = "Time (s)", ylabel = "Excitation signal", title = "HalfSine") lines!(ax, t, F) display(fig); ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](excitation_files/figure-html/cell-14-output-1.svg){} ::: ::: ### Haversine signal ::: {.api} **HaverSine** --- ::: {#28 .cell execution_count=1} ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ```{=html} 
HaverSine(F₀, tstart, duration)

Struct to define a Haversine (or versed sine) excitation signal

Fields

  • F₀: Amplitude of the force [N]

  • tstart: Starting time of the excitation [s]

  • duration: Duration of the excitation [s]

``` ::: ::: ::: ::: {#30 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.julia .cell-code} # Time parameters Δt = 1e-3 t = 0.:Δt:10. # Excitation parameters F₀ = 1. tstart = 2.5 duration = 5. # Initialize HaverSine type haversine = HaverSine(F₀, tstart, duration) # Compute the excitation F = excitation(haversine, t) # Display fig = Figure() ax = Axis(fig[1, 1], xlabel = "Time (s)", ylabel = "Excitation signal", title = "HaverSine") lines!(ax, t, F) display(fig); ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](excitation_files/figure-html/cell-16-output-1.svg){} ::: ::: ### Swept sine ::: {.api} **SweptSine** --- ::: {#32 .cell execution_count=1} ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ```{=html} 
SweptSine(F₀, tstart, duration, fstart, fend, type)

Struct to define a swept sine excitation signal

Fields

  • F₀: Amplitude of the force [N]

  • tstart: Starting time of the excitation [s]

  • duration: Duration of the excitation [s]

  • fstart: Starting frequency [Hz]

  • fend: Ending frequency [Hz]

  • type: Type of sweep

    • :lin - linear (default)

    • :quad - quadratic

    • :log - logarithmic

  • zero_end: Boolean to set the excitation to 0 at the end of the duration (default = true)

``` ::: ::: ::: ::: {#34 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.julia .cell-code} # Time parameters Δt = 1e-3 t = 0.:Δt:10. # Excitation parameters F₀ = 1. tstart = 2.5 duration = 5. fstart = 1. fend = 6. type_swept = :lin # Initialize SweptSine type chirp = SweptSine(F₀, tstart, duration, fstart, fend, type_swept, zero_end = true) # Compute the excitation F = excitation(chirp, t) # Display fig = Figure() ax = Axis(fig[1, 1], xlabel = "Time (s)", ylabel = "Excitation signal", title = "SweptSine") lines!(ax, t, F) display(fig); ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](excitation_files/figure-html/cell-18-output-1.svg){} ::: ::: ### Gaussian pulse ::: {.api} **GaussianPulse** --- ::: {#36 .cell execution_count=1} ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ```{=html} 
GaussianPulse(F₀, tstart, duration, fc)

Struct to define a Gaussian pulse excitation signal

Fields

  • F₀: Amplitude of the force [N]

  • tstart: Starting time of the excitation [s]

  • duration: Duration of the excitation [s]

  • fc: Center frequency of the pulse [Hz]

  • precision: Precision of the pulse (default = 4)

Note on the parameter precision

The precision parameter calibrates the standard deviation of the pulse, so that the duration = n x σ, within some precision. If n = 1.96 then the confidence interval of the Gaussian distribution is 95%. To do so, we compute n so that at t = duration = n x σ , the amplitude of F₀exp(-0.5(t - duration/2)^2/sigma^2) = 10^(-precision)

``` ::: ::: ::: ::: {#38 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.julia .cell-code} # Time parameters Δt = 1e-3 t = 0.:Δt:10. # Excitation parameters F₀ = 1. tstart = 2.5 duration = 5. fc = 2. # Initialize GaussianPulse type gpulse = GaussianPulse(F₀, tstart, duration, fc) # Compute the excitation F = excitation(gpulse, t) # Display fig = Figure() ax = Axis(fig[1, 1], xlabel = "Time (s)", ylabel = "Excitation signal", title = "Gaussian Pulse") lines!(ax, t, F) display(fig); ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](excitation_files/figure-html/cell-20-output-1.svg){} ::: ::: ### Colored noise ::: {.api} **ColoredNoise** --- ::: {#40 .cell execution_count=1} ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ```{=html} 
ColoredNoise(F₀, color)

Struct to define a colored noise excitation signal

Fields

  • F₀: Mean amplitude of the force [N]

  • tstart: Starting time of the excitation [s]

  • duration: Duration of the excitation [s]

  • σ: Target standard deviation of the colored noise

  • color: Color of the noise

    • :white (default)

    • :pink

    • :blue

    • :brown

    • :purple

  • band_freq: Frequencies used to defined the bandpass filter applied to the colored noise

``` ::: ::: ::: ::: {#42 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.julia .cell-code} # Time parameters Δt = 1e-4 t = 0.:Δt:10. nt = length(t) fs = 1/Δt # Excitation parameters F₀ = 1. tstart = 0. duration = 10. σ = 1. # Initialize ColoredNoise type exc_white = ColoredNoise(F₀, tstart, duration, σ, color = :white) exc_pink = ColoredNoise(F₀, tstart, duration, σ, color = :pink) exc_blue = ColoredNoise(F₀, tstart, duration, σ, color = :blue) exc_brown = ColoredNoise(F₀, tstart, duration, σ, color = :brown) exc_purple = ColoredNoise(F₀, tstart, duration, σ, color = :purple) # Compute the spectrum of the colored noise freq = rfftfreq(nt, fs) S_white = rfft(excitation(exc_white, t)) S_pink = rfft(excitation(exc_pink, t)) S_blue = rfft(excitation(exc_blue, t)) S_brown = rfft(excitation(exc_brown, t)) S_purple = rfft(excitation(exc_purple, t)) # Display fig = Figure(size = (700, 450)) ax = Axis(fig[1, 1], xscale = log10, yscale = log10, xlabel = "Frequency (Hz)", ylabel = "Amplitude", title = "Colored noise") lines!(ax, freq, abs.(S_white), label = "White noise") lines!(ax, freq, abs.(S_pink), label = "Pink noise") lines!(ax, freq, abs.(S_blue), label = "Blue noise") lines!(ax, freq, abs.(S_brown), label = "Brown noise") lines!(ax, freq, abs.(S_purple), label = "Purple noise") axislegend(ax, position = :ct, backgroundcolor = (:white, 0.5), orientation = :horizontal) display(fig); ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](excitation_files/figure-html/cell-22-output-1.svg){} ::: ::: ## `excitation` function {#sec-exc-api} ::: {.api} **excitation** --- ::: {#44 .cell execution_count=1} ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ```{=html} 
excitation(type, t)

Computes different types of excitation signals

Inputs

  • type : Excitation type

    1. Triangle

    2. Rectangle

    3. Hammer

    4. SmoothRect

    5. SineWave

    6. HalfSine

    7. HaverSine

    8. SweptSine

    9. GaussianPulse

    10. ColoredNoise

  • t: Time vector

Output

  • F: Excitation signal

``` ::: ::: :::
